
```{r}
################################################################################
# PROJECT: Structural and Temporal Constraints in Avian Erythrocytes
# SCRIPT:  04_Comparative_Nuclear_Fate_Regulation_Human_vs_Avian.R
# 
# PURPOSE: This script establishes a high-resolution human benchmark to compare
#          mammalian enucleation against avian nuclear retention.
#
# DATA SOURCE (Human): 
#   Bandyopadhyay et al. (2024), Cell (DOI: 10.1016/j.cell.2024.04.013).
#   NCBI GEO Accession: GSE253355.
#
# RATIONALE:
#   Utilizes the human bone marrow atlas (HscRNA) from femoral head tissue. 
#   Targeted sub-clustering of MEP, Erythroblast, Late Erythroid, and RBC 
#   clusters captures the full spectrum of human erythropoiesis. This serves 
#   as the basis for identifying divergent markers of enucleation across 
#   the avian and human lineages.
################################################################################


# --- 1. Load All Required Libraries ---

library(Seurat)
library(monocle3)
library(CellChat)
library(CytoTRACE)
library(scMetabolism)
library(viper)
library(dorothea)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(biomaRt)
library(org.Gg.eg.db)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(plyr)
library(magrittr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(pheatmap)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)
library(ggsci)
library(ggvenn)
library(gridExtra)
library(hdf5r)
library(openxlsx)
library(writexl)
library(psych)
library(BiocManager)
library(SeuratWrappers)

# --- 2. Directory Setup ---
# Set working directory to the project root (if not using Rproj)
# setwd("path/to/repository") 

if (!dir.exists("./Results")) dir.create(".Results")
if (!dir.exists("./Processed_Objects")) dir.create("./Processed_Objects")
if (!dir.exists("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files")) {
  dir.create("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files", recursive = TRUE)
}

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4A: HUMAN BONE MARROW REFERENCE (HscRNA) PROCESSING
# ==============================================================================

# 3. Load and Subset Human Data
# Requirement: Download GSE253355_Normal_Bone_Marrow_Atlas_Seurat_SB_v2.rds.gz 
# from GEO and place in the Raw_Data folder.
Full_data <- readRDS("./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Human_Atlas_HscRNA/GSE253355.rds")
# Targeted isolation of the hematopoietic compartment as per manuscript design
selected_clusters <- c("Erythroblast", "Late Erythroid", "MEP", "RBC")
HscRNA_1 <- subset(Full_data, idents = selected_clusters)

# 4. Processing and Dimensionality Reduction
HscRNA_1 <- NormalizeData(HscRNA_1)
HscRNA_1 <- FindVariableFeatures(HscRNA_1, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000)
HscRNA_1 <- ScaleData(HscRNA_1)
HscRNA_1 <- RunPCA(HscRNA_1, npcs = 50, verbose = FALSE)
# Plot PC1 vs PC2
pdf(file = paste0("./Results/Supplementary Figure S4-A.pdf"))  
DimPlot(HscRNA_1, reduction = "pca", dims = c(1, 2))  # Plot PC1 vs PC2
dev.off() 
# Generate an Elbow Plot for selecting PCs
pdf(file = paste0("./Results/Supplementary Figure S4-B.pdf"))  
ElbowPlot(HscRNA_1, ndims = 50)  
dev.off()  
# Perform UMAP and clustering
HscRNA_1 <- RunUMAP(HscRNA_1, dims = 1:20)
HscRNA_1 <- FindNeighbors(HscRNA_1, dims = 1:20)
HscRNA_1 <- FindClusters(HscRNA_1, resolution = 0.4)
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(1234)  # Choose any integer value
# Save UMAP plot
pdf(file = paste0("./Results/Supplementary Figure S4-D.pdf"))
DimPlot(HscRNA_1, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + ggtitle("UMAP Clustering")
dev.off()
# Save violin plots for key features
pdf(file = paste0("./Results/Supplementary Figure S4-C.pdf"), width = 10, height = 6)
vln_list <- lapply(
  c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA"),
  function(f) VlnPlot(HscRNA_1, features = f, pt.size = 0.1) + ggtitle(f)
)
p_vln <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = vln_list, ncol = 2)
print(p_vln)
dev.off()

# 5. High-Resolution Subsetting
# Subset the dataset based on specific erythroid clusters (5, 1, 2, 8, 6, 0, 4, 9, 10)
selected_clusters <- c(5, 1, 2, 8, 6, 0, 4, 9, 10)
HscRNA <- subset(HscRNA_1, idents = selected_clusters)

# 6. Marker Identification
HscRNA_markers <- FindAllMarkers(
  HscRNA, 
  only.pos = TRUE,     # Only positive markers (upregulated)
  min.pct = 0.25,      # Genes expressed in at least 25% of cells
  logfc.threshold = 0.25 # Log fold change greater than 0.25
)
# Save the top 20 markers to CSV
top20 <- HscRNA_markers %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  top_n(20, wt = avg_log2FC)
write.csv(top20, file = paste0("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/HscRNA_TOP20.csv"))

# Save the UMAP plot for the selected erythroid clusters
pdf(file = paste0("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/HscRNA_UMAP_Selected_Clusters.pdf"))
DimPlot(HscRNA, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + 
  ggtitle("UMAP of Selected Clusters") 
dev.off() 

# 7. Final Annotation Preparation
# Reorder cluster identities explicitly to reflect biological maturation
HscRNA <- SetIdent(HscRNA, 
                         value = factor(Idents(HscRNA), levels = c(5, 1, 2, 8, 6, 0, 4, 9, 10)))
# Save the primary human processed object
saveRDS(HscRNA, file = "./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4B: HUMAN MATURATION PROFILING (CytoTRACE)
# ==============================================================================

# Subset the data to the selected clusters only
HscRNA_red <- HscRNA
# Make sure the clusters are reordered
Idents(HscRNA_red) <- "seurat_clusters"
# Subset HscRNA_red for only the selected maturation clusters
selected_clusters <- c(5, 1, 2, 8, 6, 0, 4, 9, 10)
HscRNA_red <- subset(HscRNA_red, idents = selected_clusters)
# Extract the expression matrix (cells from selected clusters only)
mat <- as.matrix(HscRNA_red@assays$RNA@counts)
# Recreate phe so it matches exactly the cells in subset
phe <- as.character(Idents(HscRNA_red))
names(phe) <- colnames(HscRNA_red)

# 9. Run CytoTRACE Analysis
# Define output directory within Results
# Plot top 10 genes by CytoTRACE score
ct_output <- "./Results/HscRNA_Cytotrace"
if (!dir.exists(ct_output)) dir.create(ct_output, recursive = TRUE)
results <- CytoTRACE(mat = mat)
plotCytoGenes(results, numOfGenes = 10, outputDir = ct_output)
# Plot CytoTRACE for the gene TFRC (One of the most widely used surface markers to stage human erythroid differentiation)
plotCytoTRACE(results, phenotype = phe, gene = "TFRC", outputDir = ct_output)
# Plot CytoTRACE with phenotype labels for your selected clusters
plotCytoTRACE(results, phenotype = phe, outputDir = ct_output)
# Plot CytoTRACE summary plot for your selected clusters
plotCytoTRACE(results, phenotype = phe)

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4C: CROSS-SPECIES (Human vs Chicken) ORTHOLOGY & CORRELATION ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

cat("--- Connecting to Ensembl (Chicken dataset) ---\n")
# Connect to the Chicken (Gallus gallus) Ensembl database
chicken_mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "ggallus_gene_ensembl")
cat("--- Fetching Human Orthologs ---\n")
# Retrieve chicken gene names and their human ortholog gene names
ortholog_data <- getBM(
  attributes = c('external_gene_name', 'hsapiens_homolog_associated_gene_name'),
  mart = chicken_mart
)
#"Gene.name" (chicken) and "Human.gene.name" (human)
colnames(ortholog_data) <- c("Gene.name", "Human.gene.name")
# Remove rows where human ortholog is missing (empty strings)
ortholog_data <- ortholog_data[ortholog_data$Human.gene.name != "", ]
#Save the file#
output_path <- "./External_Data_File/human_chicken_orthologs.txt"
write.table(
  ortholog_data, 
  file = output_path, 
  sep = "\t", 
  row.names = FALSE, 
  quote = FALSE
)
cat("File successfully created at:", output_path, "\n")

# 13. Data Loading and Cluster Preparation
# To ensure a fair comparison, we synchronize the naming anddevelopmental order of erythroid clusters across both species.
cat("--- Loading and Preparing Human and Avian Data ---\n")
# Load human scRNA object
HscRNA <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")
# Load chicken + PB scRNA merged object
BMscRNA_PBscRNA <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")
# Subset and Rename Human Erythroid Clusters
new_cluster_names_human <- c(
  "0" = "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "1" = "BFU-E", "2" = "CFU-E",
  "4" = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "5" = "MEP", "6" = "Basophilic Erythroblast",
  "8" = "Proerythroblast", "9" = "Reticulocyte", "10" = "Mature erythrocyte-H"
)
Idents(HscRNA) <- dplyr::recode(Idents(HscRNA), !!!new_cluster_names_human)

selected_clusters_human <- c(
  "MEP", "BFU-E", "CFU-E", "Proerythroblast",
  "Basophilic Erythroblast", "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
  "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "Reticulocyte", "Mature erythrocyte-H"
)
HscRNA <- subset(HscRNA, idents = selected_clusters_human)

# Rename chicken clusters
new_cluster_names_chicken <- c(
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast", "1" = "Mid polychromatic blast", "2" = "Late erythroblast",
  "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 2", "4" = "Early polychromatic blast", "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 1",
  "6" = "Immature erythrocyte", "8" = "Early erythroblast", "9" = "Mature erythrocyte-C"
)
Idents(BMscRNA_PBscRNA) <- dplyr::recode(Idents(BMscRNA_PBscRNA), !!!new_cluster_names_chicken)

# 14. Correlation Matrix Construction
# Identify transcriptionally similar stages in human and chicken erythropoiesis using Spearman correlation on pseudobulk profiles.
# Process Orthologs to ensure 1-to-1 mapping
ortholog_file_path <- "./External_Data_File/human_chicken_orthologs.txt"
orthologs_raw <- read.delim(ortholog_file_path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
orthologs_one_to_one <- orthologs_raw %>%
  dplyr::rename(chicken_gene = Gene.name, human_gene = Human.gene.name) %>%
  filter(human_gene != "" & !is.na(human_gene) & chicken_gene != "" & !is.na(chicken_gene)) %>%
  distinct(human_gene, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  distinct(chicken_gene, .keep_all = TRUE)
# Generate Pseudobulk Profiles (Average Expression)
human_avg <- AverageExpression(HscRNA, assays = "RNA", slot = "data")$RNA
chicken_avg <- AverageExpression(BMscRNA_PBscRNA, assays = "RNA", slot = "data")$RNA
# Filter for common genes present in both matrices and ortholog list
final_orthologs <- orthologs_one_to_one %>%
  filter(human_gene %in% rownames(human_avg) & chicken_gene %in% rownames(chicken_avg))
human_matched <- human_avg[final_orthologs$human_gene, ]
chicken_matched <- chicken_avg[final_orthologs$chicken_gene, ]
rownames(chicken_matched) <- final_orthologs$human_gene 
# Define Biological Order for Visualization
human_order <- c("MEP", "BFU-E", "CFU-E", "Proerythroblast",
                 "Basophilic Erythroblast", "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
                 "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "Reticulocyte", "Mature erythrocyte-H")
chicken_order <- c("Erythroid progenitor 1", "Erythroid progenitor 2", 
                   "Early erythroblast", "Late erythroblast",
                   "Early polychromatic blast", "Mid polychromatic blast", 
                   "Late polychromatic blast", "Immature erythrocyte", 
                   "Mature erythrocyte-C") # Changed 'E' to 'e' to match your data
# Filter based on what is actually in the matrix
human_order <- human_order[human_order %in% colnames(human_matched)]
chicken_order <- chicken_order[chicken_order %in% colnames(chicken_matched)]
# Apply ordering
human_matched <- human_matched[, human_order, drop = FALSE]
chicken_matched <- chicken_matched[, chicken_order, drop = FALSE]
# Calculate Spearman Correlation
cat("--- Calculating Spearman correlation matrix ---\n")
cor_matrix <- cor(as.matrix(human_matched), as.matrix(chicken_matched), method = "spearman")

# 15. Visualization and Export
cat("--- Generating Heatmap ---\n")
graphics.off() 
heatmap_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("#1B9E77", "#FFFFFF", "#D95F02"))(100)
pdf("./Results/Figure-3B.pdf", width = 7, height = 6)
pheatmap(
  cor_matrix,
  color = heatmap_colors,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_format = "%.2f",
  fontsize_number = 10,
  fontsize_row = 12,
  fontsize_col = 12,
  border_color = "grey60",
  main = "Spearman Correlation: Human vs Chicken Erythroid Clusters"
)
dev.off()
# Save final matrix for manuscript tables
cat("--- Saving correlation data to Excel ---\n")
excel_sheets <- list(
  "correlation_matrix" = as.data.frame(cor_matrix)
)
write_xlsx(
  excel_sheets,
  "./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Human_Chicken_Correlation_Analysis.xlsx"
)
cat("Analysis complete. Heatmap PDF and Excel file saved.\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4D: HUMAN ERYTHROID TRAJECTORY & PSEUDOTIME (MONOCLE 3)
# ==============================================================================

# 16. Dataset Preparation for Monocle 3
# We reconstruct the human erythroid maturation trajectory to 
# serve as the mammalian benchmark for enucleation-associated gene dynamics.

# Load the processed human erythroid object
HscRNA <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")
Idents(HscRNA) <- "seurat_clusters"
selected_clusters <- c(5, 1, 2, 8, 6, 0, 4, 9, 10)
HscRNA <- subset(HscRNA, idents = selected_clusters)
# Define maturation labels and explicit factor ordering
new_cluster_names <- c(
  "0" = "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "1" = "BFU-E", "2" = "CFU-E",
  "4" = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "5" = "MEP", "6" = "Basophilic Erythroblast",
  "8" = "Proerythroblast", "9" = "Reticulocyte", "10"= "Mature erythrocyte"
)
human_cluster_order <- c(
  "MEP", "BFU-E", "CFU-E", "Proerythroblast",
  "Basophilic Erythroblast", "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
  "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "Reticulocyte", "Mature erythrocyte"
)
HscRNA$annotated_clusters <- plyr::revalue(as.character(HscRNA$seurat_clusters), new_cluster_names)
HscRNA$annotated_clusters <- factor(HscRNA$annotated_clusters, levels = human_cluster_order)

# 17. Monocle 3 Object Construction & Graph Learning
# We transfer the UMAP coordinates from Seurat to ensure trajectory alignment.
expr_matrix <- as(as.matrix(HscRNA@assays$RNA@counts), 'sparseMatrix')
cell_metadata <- HscRNA@meta.data
gene_metadata <- data.frame(gene_short_name = rownames(expr_matrix), row.names = rownames(expr_matrix))
# Create Monocle3 object
cds <- new_cell_data_set(expr_matrix, cell_metadata, gene_metadata)
cds <- preprocess_cds(cds, method = "PCA")
umap_coords <- Embeddings(HscRNA, reduction = "umap")
reducedDims(cds)$UMAP <- umap_coords
cds <- cluster_cells(cds, reduction_method = "UMAP")
# FORCE SINGLE PARTITION: Ensures the trajectory graph is built across all maturation stages
partition_vector <- factor(rep(1, ncol(cds)))
names(partition_vector) <- colnames(cds) 
colData(cds)$partition <- partition_vector 
cds@clusters$UMAP$partitions <- partition_vector 
cds@clusters$UMAP$cluster_graph <- igraph::make_empty_graph(directed = TRUE) 
# Learn trajectory graph
cds <- learn_graph(
  cds, 
  use_partition = FALSE, 
  learn_graph_control = list(euclidean_distance_ratio = 3.0) 
) 
# 18. Pseudotime Root Setting & Calculation
# We identify the earliest principal node within the MEP cluster to root the trajectory.
get_earliest_principal_node <- function(cds, cell_cluster) {
  cell_ids <- which(colData(cds)[['annotated_clusters']] == cell_cluster)
  closest_vertex <- cds@principal_graph_aux[['UMAP']]$pr_graph_cell_proj_closest_vertex
  closest_vertex <- as.matrix(closest_vertex[colnames(cds),])
  root_pr_nodes <- igraph::V(principal_graph(cds)[['UMAP']])$name[
    as.numeric(names(which.max(table(closest_vertex[cell_ids,]))))
  ]
  return(root_pr_nodes[1])
}
# Find and set root node
root_node <- get_earliest_principal_node(cds, "MEP")
cds <- order_cells(cds, reduction_method = "UMAP", root_pr_nodes = root_node)

# 19. Manual Pseudotime Assignment (Safety Net)
# Mature erythrocytes often exhibit low transcript diversity 
# which can skew automated pseudotime. We manually assign them as the terminal state.
pseudotime_data <- cds@colData %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  filter(annotated_clusters %in% c("Reticulocyte", "Orthochromatic Erythroblast"))
max_pseudotime <- max(pseudotime_data$pseudotime, na.rm = TRUE)
if (is.infinite(max_pseudotime) || is.na(max_pseudotime)) {
    max_pseudotime <- max(cds@colData$pseudotime, na.rm=TRUE) 
}
if (is.infinite(max_pseudotime) || is.na(max_pseudotime)) {
    max_pseudotime <- 10 
}
start_time_for_mature <- max_pseudotime + 1 
mature_erythrocyte_cells <- rownames(cds@colData[cds@colData$annotated_clusters == "Mature erythrocyte", ])
if (length(mature_erythrocyte_cells) > 0) {
    message(paste("Manually assigning pseudotime (value:", round(start_time_for_mature, 2), ") to", length(mature_erythrocyte_cells), "Mature Erythrocyte cells."))
    cds@colData[mature_erythrocyte_cells, "pseudotime"] <- start_time_for_mature
}

# 20. Trajectory Visualization
pdf("./Results/Supplementary Figure S5-D.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)
plot_cells(cds,
           reduction_method = "UMAP",
           color_cells_by = "annotated_clusters",
           label_groups_by_cluster = FALSE,
           label_cell_groups = FALSE,
           label_leaves = FALSE,
           label_branch_points = FALSE,
           group_label_size = 5) +
  geom_text(
    data = cds@colData %>%
      as.data.frame() %>%
      dplyr::group_by(annotated_clusters) %>%
      dplyr::summarize(UMAP_1 = median(reducedDims(cds)$UMAP[annotated_clusters == unique(annotated_clusters), 1], na.rm=TRUE),
                       UMAP_2 = median(reducedDims(cds)$UMAP[annotated_clusters == unique(annotated_clusters), 2], na.rm=TRUE)),
    aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2, label = annotated_clusters),
    size = 4, color = "black", fontface = "bold"
  )
dev.off()

# 21. Maturation Marker Dynamics
# We plot key lineage markers across pseudotime using individual scales to capture the loss of progenitor markers and gain of terminal signatures.
AFD_genes_human <- c("CD38", "CD34", "THY1", "IL3RA", "PTPRC", "CD36", "TFRC", "ENG", "KIT", "GYPA", "ITGA4", "GYPB")
AFD_genes_human_present <- AFD_genes_human[AFD_genes_human %in% rownames(cds)]
missing_genes <- setdiff(AFD_genes_human, AFD_genes_human_present)
if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
  message("These genes are not found in your dataset: ", paste(missing_genes, collapse=", "))
}
# Define gene-specific y-axis scales for high-resolution visualization
gene_scales <- list(
  "THY1" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10)),
  "TFRC" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30)),
  "PTPRC" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30)),
  "KIT" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10)),
  "ITGA4" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30)),
  "IL3RA" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30)),
  "GYPB" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10)),
  "GYPA" = list(breaks = c(1, 10, 100)), 
  "ENG" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30)),
  "CD38" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10)),
  "CD36" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30)),
  "CD34" = list(breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30))
)

default_scale <- list(breaks = c(1, 10, 100), limits = c(0.8, 100))
plots_list <- lapply(AFD_genes_human_present, function(gene) {
  gene_scale <- gene_scales[[gene]]
  if (is.null(gene_scale)) {
    gene_scale <- default_scale
    message(paste("Warning: No custom scale found for", gene, ". Using default scale."))
  } else {
      gene_scale$limits <- c(0.8, max(gene_scale$breaks))
  }
  
  p <- plot_genes_in_pseudotime(cds[gene,], color_cells_by = "annotated_clusters") + 
    scale_y_continuous(
        trans = "log10", 
        breaks = gene_scale$breaks, 
        limits = gene_scale$limits,
        labels = function(x) ifelse(x < 1, format(x, scientific = FALSE), as.character(x))
    ) +
    theme(legend.position = "right") +
    ggtitle(gene)
  return(p)
})

combined_plot <- wrap_plots(plots_list, ncol = 1)

# Save the combined plot
ggsave("./Results/Figure-3C.pdf", combined_plot, width = 10, height = 2.5 * length(plots_list))

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4E: COMPARATIVE VISUALIZATION & MARKER DISCOVERY
# ==============================================================================

# 22. Side-by-Side UMAP Visualization
# To provide a high-level comparison of the erythroid maturation 
# landscapes in avian (nucleated) and human (enucleating) systems.

# Load the Integrated Avian and Human Seurat objects
human <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")
chicken <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")
# Generate baseline UMAP plots for visualization
chicken_umap <- DimPlot(chicken, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, label.size = 4) + ggtitle("Chicken UMAP")
human_umap <- DimPlot(human, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, label.size = 4) + ggtitle("Human UMAP")
# Start PDF output for both plots side by side on the same page
pdf("./Results/Figure-3A.pdf", width = 14, height = 8)
gridExtra::grid.arrange(chicken_umap, human_umap, ncol = 2)
dev.off()

# 23. Chicken Cluster Annotation and Reordering
# We rename and reorder the avian clusters to reflect the biological maturation order from Progenitors to Mature cells.
new_cluster_names_chicken <- c(
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast",
  "1" = "Mid polychromatic blast",
  "2" = "Late erythroblast",
  "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 2",
  "4" = "Early polychromatic blast",
  "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 1",
  "6" = "Immature erythrocyte",
  "8" = "Early erythroblast",
  "9" = "Mature erythrocyte-C"
)
# Update the chicken cluster labels
Idents(chicken) <- plyr::mapvalues(Idents(chicken), from = names(new_cluster_names_chicken), to = new_cluster_names_chicken)
# Define the custom order for chicken clusters
custom_order_chicken <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-C", "Immature erythrocyte", "Late polychromatic blast", 
  "Mid polychromatic blast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Late erythroblast", 
  "Early erythroblast", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Erythroid progenitor 1"
)
Idents(chicken) <- factor(Idents(chicken), levels = custom_order_chicken)
# Save the updated chicken object
saveRDS(chicken, file = "./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")
# Rename clusters for human with desired order
new_cluster_names_human <- c(
  "5" = "MEP",
  "1" = "BFU-E",
  "2" = "CFU-E",
  "8" = "Proerythroblast",
  "6" = "Basophilic Erythroblast",
  "0" = "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
  "4" = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast",
  "9" = "Reticulocyte",
  "10" = "Mature erythrocyte-H"
)
# Update the human cluster labels
Idents(human) <- plyr::mapvalues(Idents(human), from = names(new_cluster_names_human), to = new_cluster_names_human)
# Define the custom order for human clusters
custom_order_human <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-H", "Reticulocyte", "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", 
  "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "Basophilic Erythroblast", 
  "Proerythroblast", "CFU-E", "BFU-E", "MEP"
)
# Reorder human cluster identities
Idents(human) <- factor(Idents(human), levels = custom_order_human)
# Save the updated human Seurat object
saveRDS(human, file = "./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")
# Confirm save
print("Updated Seurat objects for chicken and human have been saved with new names.")
chicken.markers <- FindAllMarkers(chicken,
  only.pos = TRUE, 
  min.pct = 0.25,
  logfc.threshold = 0.25)
human.markers <- FindAllMarkers(human,
  only.pos = TRUE, 
  min.pct = 0.25,
  logfc.threshold = 0.25)
# Save chicken markers to an Excel file
write_xlsx(chicken.markers, path = "./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/chicken_markers.xlsx")
# Save human markers to an Excel file
write_xlsx(human.markers, path = "./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/human_markers.xlsx")
# Save results to RDS files
saveRDS(chicken.markers, "./Processed_Objects/chicken_markers.rds")
saveRDS(human.markers, "./Processed_Objects/human_markers.rds")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4F: STAGE-BY-STAGE DIFFERENTIAL EXPRESSION ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

# 27. Load Updated Seurat Objects
# We use the re-annotated and re-ordered objects from SCRIPT 4E 
# to perform pairwise comparisons across the maturation timelines.
chicken <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")
human <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")

# 28. Chicken Pairwise Comparisons (Avian Maturation)
# Identify genes regulating the transition between nucleated stages.
chicken_diff_exp1 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Mature erythrocyte-C", ident.2 = "Immature erythrocyte")
chicken_diff_exp2 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Immature erythrocyte", ident.2 = "Late polychromatic blast")
chicken_diff_exp3 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Late polychromatic blast", ident.2 = "Mid polychromatic blast")
chicken_diff_exp4 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Mid polychromatic blast", ident.2 = "Early polychromatic blast")
chicken_diff_exp5 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Early polychromatic blast", ident.2 = "Late erythroblast")
chicken_diff_exp6 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Late erythroblast", ident.2 = "Early erythroblast")
chicken_diff_exp7 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Early erythroblast", ident.2 = "Erythroid progenitor 2")
chicken_diff_exp8 <- FindMarkers(chicken, ident.1 = "Erythroid progenitor 2", ident.2 = "Erythroid progenitor 1")

# 29. Human Pairwise Comparisons (Mammalian Maturation)
# Identify genes regulating mammalian erythroid progression and enucleation.
human_diff_exp1 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "Mature erythrocyte-H", ident.2 = "Reticulocyte")
human_diff_exp2 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "Reticulocyte", ident.2 = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast")
human_diff_exp3 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", ident.2 = "Polychromatic Erythroblast")
human_diff_exp4 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "Polychromatic Erythroblast", ident.2 = "Basophilic Erythroblast")
human_diff_exp5 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "Basophilic Erythroblast", ident.2 = "Proerythroblast")
human_diff_exp6 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "Proerythroblast", ident.2 = "CFU-E")
human_diff_exp7 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "CFU-E", ident.2 = "BFU-E")
human_diff_exp8 <- FindMarkers(human, ident.1 = "BFU-E", ident.2 = "MEP")

# 30. Post-processing and Excel Export
# We consolidate these findings into multi-sheet Excel workbooks, ensuring gene symbols are explicitly included as a column for readability.
# Add Gene column from row names for chicken results
chicken_results_with_genes <- lapply(list(
  "Mature vs Immature" = chicken_diff_exp1,
  "Immature vs Late poly" = chicken_diff_exp2,
  "Late poly vs Mid poly" = chicken_diff_exp3,
  "Mid poly vs Early poly" = chicken_diff_exp4,
  "Early poly vs Late erythro" = chicken_diff_exp5,
  "Late erythro vs Early erythro" = chicken_diff_exp6,
  "Early eryth vs Erythroid prog 2" = chicken_diff_exp7,
  "Erythroid prog 2 vs 1" = chicken_diff_exp8
), function(df) {
  df$Gene <- rownames(df)  # Add Gene column with row names
  return(df)
})
# Save differential expression results to Excel for chicken
write.xlsx(chicken_results_with_genes, file = paste0("./Results/Supplementary Table 6.xlsx"), overwrite = TRUE)
# Add Gene column from row names for human results
human_results_with_genes <- lapply(list(
  "Mature vs Reticulocyte" = human_diff_exp1,
  "Reticulocyte vs Orthrochromatic" = human_diff_exp2,
  "Orthrochromatic vs Poly" = human_diff_exp3,
  "Poly vs Basophilic" = human_diff_exp4,
  "Basophilic vs Proerythroblast" = human_diff_exp5,
  "Proerythroblast vs CFU-E" = human_diff_exp6,
  "CFU-E vs BFU-E" = human_diff_exp7,
  "BFU-E vs MEP" = human_diff_exp8
), function(df) {
  df$Gene <- rownames(df)  # Add Gene column with row names
  return(df)
})
# Save differential expression results to Excel for human
write.xlsx(human_results_with_genes, file = paste0("./Results/Supplementary Table 7.xlsx"), overwrite = TRUE)


# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4G: HUMAN METABOLIC PROFILING (scMetabolism)
# ==============================================================================

# 31. Metabolic Dataset Preparation
# We analyze the metabolic landscape of human erythropoiesis to understand the energetic requirements of enucleation and terminal maturation.

HscRNA <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")
DefaultAssay(HscRNA) <- "RNA" 
Idents(HscRNA) <- "seurat_clusters"
HscRNA_Mb <- HscRNA

# 32. scMetabolism Analysis Workflow
#Calculate metabolic pathway activity scores using the KEGG database and the AUCell method.
countexp.Seurat <- sc.metabolism.Seurat(obj = HscRNA_Mb, method = "AUCell", imputation = F, ncores = 6, metabolism.type = "KEGG")
metabolic.matrix <- as.data.frame(countexp.Seurat@assays$METABOLISM)
length(rownames(metabolic.matrix))

# 33. Visualization Adjustment
umap_embeddings <- Embeddings(countexp.Seurat, "umap")  
colnames(umap_embeddings) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")      
countexp.Seurat@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings <- umap_embeddings
DimPlot.metabolism(obj = countexp.Seurat, pathway = "Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis", dimention.reduction.type = 
"umap",dimention.reduction.run = F, size = 1)

# 34. Metabolic Pathway Activity 
pat <- c(
  "Sulfur metabolism",
  "Starch and sucrose metabolism",
  "Sphingolipid metabolism",
  "Pyrimidine metabolism",
  "Purine metabolism",
  "Propanoate metabolism",
  "Porphyrin and chlorophyll metabolism",
  "Oxidative phosphorylation",
  "One carbon pool by folate",
  "Nitrogen metabolism",
  "Glyoxylate and dicarboxylate metabolism",
  "Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis",
  "Glutathione metabolism",
  "Galactose metabolism",
  "Arginine and proline metabolism"
)
# Define cluster names
cluster_names <- c(
  "5" = "MEP",
  "1" = "BFU-E",
  "2" = "CFU-E",
  "8" = "Proerythroblast",
  "6" = "Basophilic Erythroblast",
  "0" = "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
  "4" = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast",
  "9" = "Reticulocyte",
  "10" = "Mature erythrocyte"
)
# Save the plot to a PDF file
pdf("./Results/Figure-5B.pdf", width = 10, height = 5)
# Generate the dot plot with cluster names
DotPlot.metabolism(
    obj = countexp.Seurat, 
    pathway = pat, 
    phenotype = "seurat_clusters", 
    norm = "y"
) + 
    # Reorder clusters on the x-axis with names
    scale_x_discrete(limits = names(cluster_names), labels = cluster_names)
# Close the PDF device
dev.off()

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4H: COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS OF ENUCLEATION MACHINERY 
# ==============================================================================

# 35. Dataset Loading and Maturation Synchronization
# We reload and explicitly re-order both datasets to ensure the terminal maturation stages (Mature erythrocyte-C/H) are correctly 
# positioned for cross-species comparison.
chicken <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")
human <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds")
# Rename clusters for chicken with desired order
new_cluster_names_chicken <- c(
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast",
  "1" = "Mid polychromatic blast",
  "2" = "Late erythroblast",
  "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 2",
  "4" = "Early polychromatic blast",
  "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 1",
  "6" = "Immature erythrocyte",
  "8" = "Early erythroblast",
  "9" = "Mature erythrocyte-C"
)
# Define the custom order for chicken clusters
custom_order_chicken <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-C", "Immature erythrocyte", "Late polychromatic blast", 
  "Mid polychromatic blast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Late erythroblast", 
  "Early erythroblast", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Erythroid progenitor 1"
)
# Reorder chicken cluster identities
Idents(chicken) <- factor(Idents(chicken), levels = custom_order_chicken)
# Rename clusters for human with desired order
new_cluster_names_human <- c(
  "5" = "MEP",
  "1" = "BFU-E",
  "2" = "CFU-E",
  "8" = "Proerythroblast",
  "6" = "Basophilic Erythroblast",
  "0" = "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
  "4" = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast",
  "9" = "Reticulocyte",
  "10" = "Mature erythrocyte-H"
)

# Update the human cluster labels
Idents(human) <- plyr::mapvalues(Idents(human), from = names(new_cluster_names_human), to = new_cluster_names_human)
# Define the custom order for human clusters
custom_order_human <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-H", "Reticulocyte", "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", 
  "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "Basophilic Erythroblast", 
  "Proerythroblast", "CFU-E", "BFU-E", "MEP"
)
# Reorder human cluster identities
Idents(human) <- factor(Idents(human), levels = custom_order_human)
# Load the saved results
chicken.markers <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/chicken_markers.rds")
human.markers <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/human_markers.rds")

# 36. Comparative DotPlot Generation (Figure 4A)
# Visualize the expression dynamics of Rho GTPases (enucleation drivers), Cytoskeletal components (Vimentin/Actin), and Caspases across both species.
# Set default assay
DefaultAssay(chicken) <- "SCT"
# Curated list of genes related to enucleation and cytoskeletal architecture
key_genes <- c("RHOA","RAC1","RAC2","DIAPH2","ACTB","ACTG1","PHOSPHO1","VIM","ACTN2","TPM3","DES","UBAC1","ZEB1","CDH2","CASP3","CASP6","CASP8","CASP7")
# Ensure cluster order is respected
custom_order_human <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-H", "Reticulocyte", "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", 
  "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "Basophilic Erythroblast", 
  "Proerythroblast", "CFU-E", "BFU-E", "MEP"
)
custom_order_chicken <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-C", "Immature erythrocyte", "Late polychromatic blast", 
  "Mid polychromatic blast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Late erythroblast", 
  "Early erythroblast", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Erythroid progenitor 1"
)
Idents(human) <- factor(Idents(human), levels = custom_order_human)
Idents(chicken) <- factor(Idents(chicken), levels = custom_order_chicken)
# Human DotPlot: Orange-Red gradient to signify the mammalian lineage
dotplot_human <- DotPlot(human, features = key_genes) +
  ggtitle("Human (Enucleating)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
  labs(x = "Genes", y = "Erythroid Clusters") +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "lightgrey", mid = "orange", high = "red", midpoint = 1)
# Chicken DotPlot: Skyblue-Blue gradient to signify the avian lineage
dotplot_chicken <- DotPlot(chicken, features = key_genes) +
  ggtitle("Chicken (Non-enucleating)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
  labs(x = "Genes", y = "Erythroid Clusters") +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "lightgrey", mid = "skyblue", high = "blue", midpoint = 1)
# Combine and export side-by-side plot
pdf("./Results/Figure-4A.pdf", width = 16, height = 6)
dotplot_human + dotplot_chicken + plot_layout(ncol = 2)
dev.off()

# 37. EXPORT SOURCE DATA FOR MANUSCRIPT 
# To satisfy journal requirements for "Source Data" and to provide 
# numerical proof of the divergent expression of Rho GTPases and Vimentin.
# Define genes
cat("--- Extracting numerical Source Data for Figure 4A --- \n")
# Define the genes used in the plot
genes_figure_4a <- c("RHOA","RAC1","RAC2","DIAPH2","ACTB","ACTG1","PHOSPHO1",
                     "VIM","ACTN2","TPM3","DES","UBAC1","ZEB1","CDH2",
                     "CASP3","CASP6","CASP8","CASP7")
# Extract the data frame that Seurat uses to build the DotPlot
# This contains 'avg.exp', 'pct.exp', and 'avg.exp.scaled'
dot_data_human <- DotPlot(human, features = genes_figure_4a)$data
dot_data_chicken <- DotPlot(chicken, features = genes_figure_4a)$data
# Save to Excel as a formal Source Data file
library(openxlsx)
write.xlsx(list(Human_Source_Data = dot_data_human, 
                Chicken_Source_Data = dot_data_chicken),
           file = "./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Source_Data_Figure_4A.xlsx")
cat("Source data successfully exported to: Source_Data_Figure_4A.xlsx \n")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4I: COMPARATIVE GENE EXPRESSION & REGULATORY FATE
# ==============================================================================

# 38. Setup and Data Loading
# We analyze the relationship between Vimentin (VIM) persistence and 
# cell death regulation to characterize the avian "Build to Last" strategy.

# Load Seurat objects and associated markers
chicken_rds_path <- "./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds"
human_rds_path <- "./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds"
chicken_markers_path <- "./Processed_Objects/chicken_markers.rds"
human_markers_path <- "./Processed_Objects/human_markers.rds"

# Load Seurat objects and marker lists
chicken <- readRDS(chicken_rds_path)
human <- readRDS(human_rds_path)
chicken.markers <- readRDS(chicken_markers_path)
human.markers <- readRDS(human_markers_path)

#Cluster Renaming and Ordering - Chicken
custom_order_chicken <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-C", "Immature erythrocyte", "Late polychromatic blast",
  "Mid polychromatic blast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Late erythroblast",
  "Early erythroblast", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Erythroid progenitor 1"
)
chicken$cell_type <- plyr::mapvalues(
    x = chicken$seurat_clusters,
    from = c(9, 6, 0, 1, 4, 2, 8, 3, 5),
    to = custom_order_chicken
)
Idents(chicken) <- factor(chicken$cell_type, levels = custom_order_chicken)
#Cluster Renaming and Ordering - Human
new_cluster_names_human <- c(
  "5" = "MEP", "1" = "BFU-E", "2" = "CFU-E", "8" = "Proerythroblast",
  "6" = "Basophilic Erythroblast", "0" = "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
  "4" = "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "9" = "Reticulocyte", "10" = "Mature erythrocyte-H"
)
human$cell_type <- plyr::mapvalues(human$seurat_clusters, from = names(new_cluster_names_human), to = new_cluster_names_human)
custom_order_human <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-H", "Reticulocyte", "Orthochromatic Erythroblast",
  "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "Basophilic Erythroblast",
  "Proerythroblast", "CFU-E", "BFU-E", "MEP"
)
Idents(human) <- factor(human$cell_type, levels = custom_order_human)

cat("Setup complete. Data loaded and output paths are set.\n")

# 39. Validation of Vimentin (VIM) Expression
# Demonstrate the stark divergence in VIM expression between avian 
# terminal maturation (persistence) and human maturation (loss).
cat("\n--- Running Part 1: Validating VIM Expression ---\n")
p_vim_chicken <- VlnPlot(chicken, features = "VIM", pt.size = 0) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Chicken: VIM Expression", y = "Expression Level")
p_vim_human <- VlnPlot(human, features = "VIM", pt.size = 0) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Human: VIM Expression", y = "Expression Level")
print(p_vim_chicken | p_vim_human)
p_umap_vim_chicken <- FeaturePlot(chicken, features = "VIM", pt.size = 0.5) + labs(title = "Chicken: VIM on UMAP")
p_umap_vim_human <- FeaturePlot(human, features = "VIM", pt.size = 0.5) + labs(title = "Human: VIM on UMAP")
print(p_umap_vim_chicken | p_umap_vim_human)
cat("Generated and saved plots for VIM expression.\n")
pdf("./Results/Figure_4B-C.pdf",
    width = 14, height = 7)
print(p_vim_chicken | p_vim_human)
print(p_umap_vim_chicken | p_umap_vim_human)
dev.off()


# 40. Cell Death Signature Scoring
# Using AddModuleScore to quantify the collective activity of 
# apoptotic and cell death genes (CASP3, CASP8, BCL2, etc.) across the lineage.
cat("\n--- Running Part 2: Calculating Cell Death Signature Score ---\n")
# Define the Cell Death gene signature
cell_death_genes <- c("CASP3", "CASP8", "CASP9", "NLRP3", "PYCARD", "BCL2", "FAS")
chicken_death_genes_present <- list(intersect(cell_death_genes, rownames(chicken)))
human_death_genes_present <- list(intersect(cell_death_genes, rownames(human)))
chicken <- AddModuleScore(object = chicken, features = chicken_death_genes_present, ctrl = 5, name = "CellDeathScore")
human <- AddModuleScore(object = human, features = human_death_genes_present, ctrl = 5, name = "CellDeathScore")
p_score_chicken <- VlnPlot(chicken, features = "CellDeathScore1", pt.size = 0) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Chicken: Cell Death Signature Score", y = "Signature Score")
p_score_human <- VlnPlot(human, features = "CellDeathScore1", pt.size = 0) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Human: Cell Death Signature Score", y = "Signature Score")
print(p_score_chicken | p_score_human)
cat("Generated and saved plots for Cell Death Signature Score.\n")

# 41. Correlation Analysis (Avian Persistence Mechanism)
# To quantitatively prove that high VIM expression in chicken correlates with specific cell death profiles, supporting nuclear retention.
cat("\n--- Running Part 3: Correlating VIM and Cell Death Score ---\n")
p_scatter <- FeatureScatter(object = chicken, feature1 = "VIM", feature2 = "CellDeathScore1", group.by = "ident") +
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm', formula = y ~ x, color = "black", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Chicken: VIM vs. Cell Death Score", x = "VIM Expression", y = "Cell Death Signature Score")
print(p_scatter)
cat("Generated and saved scatter plot of VIM vs. Cell Death Score.\n")

#Create a clean metadata dataframe for statistics and export
chicken_metadata <- data.frame(
  VIM = FetchData(chicken, vars = "VIM")[,1],
  CellDeathScore1 = chicken$CellDeathScore1,
  ident = Idents(chicken)
)
#Perform correlation tests and build a results table ---
stats_list <- list()
# Overall correlation
overall_corr <- cor.test(chicken_metadata$VIM, chicken_metadata$CellDeathScore1)
stats_list$Overall <- data.frame(
    Analysis = "Overall (All Cells)",
    Correlation_Coefficient_r = overall_corr$estimate,
    P_value = overall_corr$p.value
)
# Correlation in Mature Erythrocytes
mature_data <- filter(chicken_metadata, ident == "Mature erythrocyte-C")
if(nrow(mature_data) > 2) {
  mature_corr <- cor.test(mature_data$VIM, mature_data$CellDeathScore1)
  stats_list$Mature <- data.frame(
      Analysis = "Mature erythrocyte-C",
      Correlation_Coefficient_r = mature_corr$estimate,
      P_value = mature_corr$p.value
  )
}
# Correlation in Immature Erythrocytes
immature_data <- filter(chicken_metadata, ident == "Immature erythrocyte")
if(nrow(immature_data) > 2){
  immature_corr <- cor.test(immature_data$VIM, immature_data$CellDeathScore1)
  stats_list$Immature <- data.frame(
      Analysis = "Immature erythrocyte",
      Correlation_Coefficient_r = immature_corr$estimate,
      P_value = immature_corr$p.value
  )
}
#Combine all stats results into a single dataframe
correlation_stats_df <- bind_rows(stats_list)
print("Correlation Statistics:")
print(correlation_stats_df)

# 42. Data Export
# Save all numerical results to Supplementary Table 8 for manuscript documentation.
cat("\n--- Running Part 4: Exporting all data to a single Excel file ---\n")
# Create a named list of the dataframes to be written to Excel
# The names of the list will become the names of the Excel sheets
list_of_datasets <- list(
  "Correlation_Stats" = correlation_stats_df,
  "Chicken_Metadata_For_Plot" = chicken_metadata,
  "Chicken_Cluster_Markers" = chicken.markers,
  "Human_Cluster_Markers" = human.markers
)

# Define the output Excel file path
output_xlsx_path <- "./Results/Supplementary Table 8.xlsx"

# Write the list to a single .xlsx file
write.xlsx(list_of_datasets, file = output_xlsx_path, rowNames = FALSE)

cat(paste("Successfully exported all data to:", output_xlsx_path, "\n"))

# Close the PDF device, which saves the file to disk
dev.off()

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4J: COMPARATIVE TRAJECTORY DYNAMICS 
# ==============================================================================

# 43. Setup and Data Synchronization
# We utilize Monocle 3 to reconstruct maturation trajectories for both chicken and human. This allows us to compare gene #expression kinetics on a unified developmental axis.
chicken_rds_path <- "./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds"
human_rds_path <- "./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds"
output_pdf_path <- file.path("./Results/Figure-4D.pdf")
output_xlsx_path <- file.path("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Trajectory_Plot_Data.xlsx") 
if (!is.null(dev.list())) dev.off()
pdf(output_pdf_path, width = 14, height = 10)
chicken <- readRDS(chicken_rds_path)
human <- readRDS(human_rds_path)
custom_order_chicken <- c("Mature erythrocyte-C", "Immature erythrocyte", "Late polychromatic blast", "Mid polychromatic blast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Late erythroblast", "Early erythroblast", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Erythroid progenitor 1")
chicken$cell_type <- plyr::mapvalues(x = chicken$seurat_clusters, from = c(9, 6, 0, 1, 4, 2, 8, 3, 5), to = custom_order_chicken)
Idents(chicken) <- factor(chicken$cell_type, levels = custom_order_chicken)
new_cluster_names_human <- c("5"="MEP", "1"="BFU-E", "2"="CFU-E", "8"="Proerythroblast", "6"="Basophilic Erythroblast", "0"="Polychromatic Erythroblast", "4"="Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "9"="Reticulocyte", "10"="Mature erythrocyte-H")
human$cell_type <- plyr::mapvalues(human$seurat_clusters, from = names(new_cluster_names_human), to = new_cluster_names_human)
custom_order_human <- c("Mature erythrocyte-H", "Reticulocyte", "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "Basophilic Erythroblast", "Proerythroblast", "CFU-E", "BFU-E", "MEP")
Idents(human) <- factor(human$cell_type, levels = custom_order_human)
cat("Setup complete. Data loaded.\n")

# 44. Monocle 3 Trajectory Reconstruction Function
# This function automates the conversion from Seurat to Monocle 3, handles Seurat v5 layer merging (sanitization), and calculates #the principal graph for maturation.
run_monocle3_trajectory <- function(seurat_obj, object_name, root_cluster) {
  cat(paste("\n--- Starting Monocle 3 Trajectory Analysis for:", object_name, "---\n"))
  if ("Assay5" %in% class(seurat_obj[["RNA"]])) {
    count_layers <- grep("^counts\\.", Layers(seurat_obj[["RNA"]]), value = TRUE)
    if (length(count_layers) > 1) {
      cat("Sanitizing complex Seurat v5 object...\n")
      list_of_counts <- lapply(count_layers, function(layer) LayerData(seurat_obj, assay = "RNA", layer = layer))
      merged_counts <- SeuratObject:::RowMergeSparseMatrices(list_of_counts[[1]], list_of_counts[2:length(list_of_counts)])
      sanitized_seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = merged_counts, meta.data = seurat_obj@meta.data)
      sanitized_seurat <- NormalizeData(sanitized_seurat)
      sanitized_seurat <- FindVariableFeatures(sanitized_seurat)
      sanitized_seurat <- ScaleData(sanitized_seurat)
      sanitized_seurat <- RunPCA(sanitized_seurat)
      set.seed(1234) 
      sanitized_seurat <- RunUMAP(sanitized_seurat, dims = 1:20)
      seurat_obj <- sanitized_seurat
    }
  }
  cds <- as.cell_data_set(seurat_obj)
  set.seed(1234) 
  cds <- cluster_cells(cds, reduction_method = "UMAP")
  cat("Learning trajectory graph...\n")
  set.seed(1234)
  cds <- learn_graph(cds, use_partition = FALSE)
  print(plot_cells(cds, color_cells_by = "ident", label_groups_by_cluster = FALSE, group_label_size = 4))
  root_cells <- colnames(cds)[colData(cds)$ident == root_cluster]
  if (length(root_cells) == 0) {
    root_cells <- colnames(cds)[colData(cds)$cell_type == root_cluster]
  }
  if (length(root_cells) == 0) {
    stop(paste("Error: Could not find any cells for cluster:", root_cluster))
  }
  cds <- order_cells(cds, root_cells = root_cells)
  p <- plot_cells(cds, color_cells_by = "pseudotime", show_trajectory_graph = TRUE, graph_label_size = 4, label_cell_groups = FALSE, label_leaves = FALSE, label_branch_points = FALSE) +
    labs(title = paste("Monocle 3 Trajectory for", object_name)) +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
  print(p)
  cat("Generated and saved trajectory plot.\n")
  return(cds)
}
# 45. Execute Trajectory Calculations
chicken_cds <- run_monocle3_trajectory(chicken, "Chicken", "Erythroid progenitor 1")
human_cds <- run_monocle3_trajectory(human, "Human", "MEP")

# 46. Comparative Gene Expression Curves 
# Using Loess smoothing to compare the expression kinetics of structural genes (VIM, ACTB) and regulatory genes (BCL2, CASP3).
cat("\n--- Part 3: Generating the comparative gene expression vs. pseudotime plot ---\n")
genes_to_plot <- c("HBA1", "TFRC","VIM", "BCL2", "CASP3", "RHOA","DIAPH2","ACTB")
extract_pseudotime_data <- function(cds_obj, species_name) {
  if(!("gene_short_name" %in% names(rowData(cds_obj)))){ rowData(cds_obj)$gene_short_name <- rownames(cds_obj) }
  genes_present <- intersect(genes_to_plot, rowData(cds_obj)$gene_short_name)
  pt_data <- data.frame(pseudotime = pseudotime(cds_obj), t(as.matrix(exprs(cds_obj)[genes_present,])))
  pt_data_finite <- pt_data %>% dplyr::filter(is.finite(pseudotime))
  pt_data_long <- pt_data_finite %>% tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -pseudotime, names_to = "gene", values_to = "expression") %>% mutate(species = species_name)
  return(pt_data_long)
}
combined_df <- bind_rows(
  extract_pseudotime_data(chicken_cds, "Chicken"),
  extract_pseudotime_data(human_cds, "Human")
)
comparative_plot <- ggplot(combined_df, aes(x = pseudotime, y = expression, color = species)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, span = 0.6) + 
  facet_wrap(~ gene, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Chicken" = "#d95f02", "Human" = "#1b9e77")) +
  labs(title = "Comparative Gene Expression Along Developmental Trajectory (Monocle 3)", subtitle = "Chicken ('Build to Last') vs. Human ('Designed for Disassembly')", x = "Developmental Pseudotime", y = "Smoothed Gene Expression") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) + theme(legend.position = "bottom", strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
print(comparative_plot)
cat("Generated and saved the final comparative plot.\n")

# 47. Export Final Trajectory Data
# Export the numerical smoothed data to Excel for Source Data and permanent project records.
cat("\n--- Part 4: Exporting the trajectory data to an Excel file ---\n")
write.xlsx(combined_df, file = output_xlsx_path, rowNames = FALSE)
cat(paste("Successfully exported trajectory data to:", output_xlsx_path, "\n"))

dev.off()
cat(paste0("\n\n--- TRAJECTORY ANALYSIS COMPLETE ---\n"))
cat(paste0("All plots have been saved to: ", output_pdf_path, "\n"))

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4K: FUNCTIONAL ENRICHMENT & DEVELOPMENTAL OVERLAP ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

# 48. Configuration & Global Enrichment Settings
# We perform high-resolution functional profiling of the stage-specific DEGs identified in Tables 6 and 7 to characterize the #pathways driving erythropoiesis in both species (Human - Chicken).

options(timeout = 300)
human_file <- "./Results/Supplementary Table 7.xlsx"
chicken_file <- "./Results/Supplementary Table 6.xlsx"
P_ADJ_CUTOFF <- 0.05
LOGFC_CUTOFF <- 0.5

# 49. Define the Unified Enrichment Pipeline Function
# This function handles the ID conversion (Symbol to Entrez), 
# executes GO/KEGG enrichment for Up and Downregulated genes per stage, 
# and tracks mapping statistics to ensure data quality.
perform_full_enrichment_analysis <- function(file_path, organism_db, organism_kegg_code) {
  sheet_names <- getSheetNames(file_path)
  gene_lists_up <- list()
  gene_lists_down <- list()
  mapping_stats <- list()
  all_degs_list <- list()
    for (sheet in sheet_names) {
    message(paste("Processing Sheet:", sheet))
    tryCatch({
      deg_data <- read.xlsx(file_path, sheet = sheet)
      if(!all(c("p_val_adj", "avg_log2FC", "Gene") %in% names(deg_data))) next
      all_degs <- deg_data %>% filter(p_val_adj < P_ADJ_CUTOFF, abs(avg_log2FC) > LOGFC_CUTOFF) %>% pull(Gene)
      all_degs_list[[sheet]] <- all_degs
      genes_up <- deg_data %>% filter(p_val_adj < P_ADJ_CUTOFF, avg_log2FC > LOGFC_CUTOFF) %>% pull(Gene)
      if (length(genes_up) > 0) {
        entrez_up <- bitr(genes_up, fromType="SYMBOL", toType="ENTREZID", OrgDb=organism_db, drop = TRUE)
        gene_lists_up[[sheet]] <- entrez_up$ENTREZID
        mapped_genes_up <- entrez_up$SYMBOL
        unmapped_genes_up <- setdiff(genes_up, mapped_genes_up)
        mapping_stats[[paste(sheet, "Up")]] <- data.frame(
          Comparison = paste(sheet, "Up"),
          Total_DEGs = length(genes_up),
          Mapped_DEGs = length(mapped_genes_up),
          Unmapped_DEGs = length(unmapped_genes_up),
          Unmapped_Gene_List = paste(unmapped_genes_up, collapse = ", ")
        )
      }
      genes_down <- deg_data %>% filter(p_val_adj < P_ADJ_CUTOFF, avg_log2FC < -LOGFC_CUTOFF) %>% pull(Gene)
      if (length(genes_down) > 0) {
        entrez_down <- bitr(genes_down, fromType="SYMBOL", toType="ENTREZID", OrgDb=organism_db, drop = TRUE)
        gene_lists_down[[sheet]] <- entrez_down$ENTREZID
        mapped_genes_down <- entrez_down$SYMBOL
        unmapped_genes_down <- setdiff(genes_down, mapped_genes_down)
        mapping_stats[[paste(sheet, "Down")]] <- data.frame(
          Comparison = paste(sheet, "Down"),
          Total_DEGs = length(genes_down),
          Mapped_DEGs = length(mapped_genes_down),
          Unmapped_DEGs = length(unmapped_genes_down),
          Unmapped_Gene_List = paste(unmapped_genes_down, collapse = ", ")
        )
      }
    }, error = function(e) { warning(paste("Error processing sheet", sheet, ":", e$message)) })
  }
  full_mapping_stats <- do.call(rbind, mapping_stats)
  run_compare_cluster <- function(gene_list, fun_type, ...) {
    if (length(gene_list) > 0 && sum(lengths(gene_list)) > 1) {
      compareCluster(geneCluster = gene_list, fun = fun_type, ...)
    } else { NULL }
  }
  kegg_up <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_up, "enrichKEGG", organism = organism_kegg_code)
  go_bp_up <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_up, "enrichGO", OrgDb = organism_db, ont = "BP", readable = TRUE)
  go_mf_up <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_up, "enrichGO", OrgDb = organism_db, ont = "MF", readable = TRUE)
  go_cc_up <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_up, "enrichGO", OrgDb = organism_db, ont = "CC", readable = TRUE)
  kegg_down <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_down, "enrichKEGG", organism = organism_kegg_code)
  go_bp_down <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_down, "enrichGO", OrgDb = organism_db, ont = "BP", readable = TRUE)
  go_mf_down <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_down, "enrichGO", OrgDb = organism_db, ont = "MF", readable = TRUE)
  go_cc_down <- run_compare_cluster(gene_lists_down, "enrichGO", OrgDb = organism_db, ont = "CC", readable = TRUE)
  return(list(
    enrichment_results = list(
      kegg_up=kegg_up, go_bp_up=go_bp_up, go_mf_up=go_mf_up, go_cc_up=go_cc_up,
      kegg_down=kegg_down, go_bp_down=go_bp_down, go_mf_down=go_mf_down, go_cc_down=go_cc_down
    ),
    mapping_stats = full_mapping_stats,
    all_degs = all_degs_list
  ))
}

# 50. Execute Comparative Analysis
message("--- Starting Full Human Analysis ---")
human_full_results <- perform_full_enrichment_analysis(file_path = human_file, organism_db = org.Hs.eg.db, organism_kegg_code = "hsa")
message("\n--- Starting Full Chicken Analysis ---")
chicken_full_results <- perform_full_enrichment_analysis(file_path = chicken_file, organism_db = org.Gg.eg.db, organism_kegg_code = "gga")

# 51. Export Full Results and Mapping QC (Tables 9 & 10)
# Save the comprehensive functional data to Excel workbooks.
write_results_to_excel <- function(enrich_results, filename) {
  wb <- createWorkbook()
  add_sheet_safely <- function(sheet_name, data_object) {
    if (!is.null(data_object) && nrow(as.data.frame(data_object)) > 0) {
      addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
      writeData(wb, sheet_name, as.data.frame(data_object))
    }
  }
  all_sheets <- names(enrich_results)
  for(sheet_name in all_sheets) { add_sheet_safely(sheet_name, enrich_results[[sheet_name]]) }
  
  if (length(names(wb)) > 0) { saveWorkbook(wb, filename, overwrite = TRUE); message(paste(filename, "has been saved.")) }
}
message("\nWriting enrichment results to Excel files...")
write_results_to_excel(human_full_results$enrichment_results, "./Results/Supplementary Table 10.xlsx")
write_results_to_excel(chicken_full_results$enrichment_results, "./Results/Supplementary Table 9.xlsx")

# Save Mapping Statistics for QC documentation
mapping_wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(mapping_wb, "Human_Mapping_Stats")
writeData(mapping_wb, "Human_Mapping_Stats", human_full_results$mapping_stats)
addWorksheet(mapping_wb, "Chicken_Mapping_Stats")
writeData(mapping_wb, "Chicken_Mapping_Stats", chicken_full_results$mapping_stats)
saveWorkbook(mapping_wb, "./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Gene_Mapping_Statistics.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
message("Gene_Mapping_Statistics.xlsx has been saved.")

# 52. Generate Top 20 Pathway DotPlots (Supplementary Figures S8 & S9)
create_dotplot_manual_topN <- function(result_object, plot_title, n_top = 20) {
  if (is.null(result_object) || nrow(as.data.frame(result_object)) == 0) {
    return(ggplot() + annotate("text", x=1, y=1, label=paste("No significant results for:\n", plot_title), size=5) + theme_void() + ggtitle(plot_title) + theme(plot.title=element_text(hjust=0.5)))
  }
  result_df <- as.data.frame(result_object)
  top_terms <- result_df %>% arrange(p.adjust) %>% distinct(Description) %>% slice_head(n = n_top) %>% pull(Description)
  dotplot(result_object, showCategory = top_terms, font.size = 10) + 
    ggtitle(plot_title) + theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title=element_text(hjust=0.5, size=14, face="bold"), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45, hjust=1, size=10), axis.text.y=element_text(size=10))
}
generate_enrichment_pdf <- function(results_list, species_name, filename) {
  pdf(filename, width = 16, height = 12)
  plot_titles <- c("KEGG (Up)", "GO:BP (Up)", "GO:MF (Up)", "GO:CC (Up)", "KEGG (Down)", "GO:BP (Down)", "GO:MF (Down)", "GO:CC (Down)")
  for (i in 1:length(results_list)) {
    full_title <- paste(species_name, ": Top 20 ", plot_titles[i], sep="")
    print(create_dotplot_manual_topN(results_list[[i]], full_title, n_top = 20))
  }
  dev.off()
  message(paste(filename, "has been saved."))
}
generate_enrichment_pdf(chicken_full_results$enrichment_results, "Chicken", "./Results/Supplementary Figure S8.pdf")
generate_enrichment_pdf(human_full_results$enrichment_results, "Human", "./Results/Supplementary Figure S9.pdf")

# 53. Generate DEG Overlap Venn Diagrams (Supplementary Figures S6 & S7)
# Visualize transcriptomic similarity between sequential maturation 
# stages to quantify developmental continuity.
generate_venn_pdf <- function(deg_list, species_name, filename) {
  message(paste("\nGenerating Venn Diagram PDF:", filename))
  pdf(filename, width = 10, height = 10)
  stage_names <- names(deg_list)
  for (i in 1:(length(stage_names) - 1)) {
    list1_name <- stage_names[i]
    list2_name <- stage_names[i+1]
    venn_data <- list(
      A = deg_list[[list1_name]],
      B = deg_list[[list2_name]]
    )
    names(venn_data) <- c(list1_name, list2_name)
    p <- ggvenn(
      venn_data, 
      fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF"),
      stroke_size = 0.5, set_name_size = 4
    ) + ggtitle(paste(species_name, "DEG Overlap:", list1_name, "vs", list2_name)) +
        theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size=12))
    print(p)
  }
  dev.off()
  message(paste(filename, "has been saved."))
}
generate_venn_pdf(chicken_full_results$all_degs, "Chicken", "./Results/Supplementary Figure S6.pdf")
generate_venn_pdf(human_full_results$all_degs, "Human", "./Results/Supplementary Figure S7.pdf")
message("\nAll analyses and outputs are complete!")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4L: HUMAN HEMATOPOIETIC NICHE SIGNALING
# ==============================================================================

# 54. Project Setup and Path Configuration
# We utilize the full Bandyopadhyay atlas to extract myeloid "support" 
# cells and the focused HscRNA object for the erythroid "target" cells.

# Define Input Paths
full_human_data_path <- "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Human_Atlas_HscRNA/GSE253355.rds"
erythroid_only_path  <- "./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds"

# Define Output Paths
final_plots_pdf     <- file.path("./Results/Figure-5C-D.pdf")
final_results_excel <- file.path("./Results/Supplementary Table 11.xlsx")

cat("Setup complete. Paths are set for the final focused analysis.\n")


# 55. Prepare the Focused Niche Object
# Create a unified Seurat object containing myeloid niche cells and the early stages of the erythroid maturation trajectory.
cat("\n--- Part 1: Preparing a focused Myeloid + Erythroid object ---\n")
full_b_marrow <- readRDS(full_human_data_path)
myeloid_names <- c("Macrophages", "Monocyte", "Late Myeloid", "Early Myeloid Progenitor", "GMP") 
myeloid_cells <- subset(full_b_marrow, subset = cluster_anno_l2 %in% myeloid_names)
myeloid_cells$cell_type_final <- "Myeloid"
rm(full_b_marrow); gc()
erythroid_lineage <- readRDS(erythroid_only_path)
new_cluster_names_human <- c("5"="MEP", "1"="BFU-E", "2"="CFU-E")
erythroid_lineage$cell_type_final <- plyr::mapvalues(
  erythroid_lineage$seurat_clusters, 
  from = names(new_cluster_names_human), 
  to = new_cluster_names_human,
  warn_missing = FALSE
)
early_erythroid_cells <- subset(erythroid_lineage, subset = cell_type_final %in% c("MEP", "BFU-E", "CFU-E"))
DefaultAssay(myeloid_cells) <- "RNA"; DefaultAssay(early_erythroid_cells) <- "RNA"
myeloid_cells <- NormalizeData(myeloid_cells); early_erythroid_cells <- NormalizeData(early_erythroid_cells)
merged_obj <- merge(myeloid_cells, y = early_erythroid_cells)

# 56. Run CellChat Analysis Workflow
# Identify over-expressed ligands and receptors and calculate communication probabilities between the niche and progenitors.
cat("\n--- Part 2: Running CellChat on the focused dataset ---\n")
data.input <- GetAssayData(merged_obj, assay = "RNA", layer = "data") 
meta <- merged_obj@meta.data
cell_group_order <- c("Myeloid", "MEP", "BFU-E", "CFU-E")
meta$cell_chat_group <- factor(meta$cell_type_final, levels = cell_group_order)
cellchat <- createCellChat(object = data.input, meta = meta, group.by = "cell_chat_group")
CellChatDB <- CellChatDB.human
cellchat@DB <- subsetDB(CellChatDB, search = "Secreted Signaling") 
cellchat <- subsetData(cellchat)
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedGenes(cellchat)
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedInteractions(cellchat)
cellchat <- computeCommunProb(cellchat)
cellchat <- filterCommunication(cellchat, min.cells = 10)
cellchat <- computeCommunProbPathway(cellchat)
cellchat <- aggregateNet(cellchat)
cat("CellChat analysis complete.\n")

# 57. Niche Visualization . Visualize the overall interaction strength and specific 
# ligand-receptor pairs bridging myeloid cells and erythroid progenitors.
cat("\n--- Part 3: Visualizing results and exporting to Excel ---\n")
pdf(final_plots_pdf, width = 12, height = 10, onefile = TRUE)
all_interactions_df <- subsetCommunication(cellchat)
pval_threshold <- 0.01
significant_interactions_df <- all_interactions_df %>%
  filter(pval < pval_threshold)
cat(paste("Found", nrow(significant_interactions_df), "interactions with p-value <", pval_threshold, "\n"))
netVisual_circle(cellchat@net$weight, vertex.weight = as.numeric(table(cellchat@meta$cell_chat_group)), 
                 weight.scale = T, label.edge = F, title.name = "Interaction Strength - Myeloid to Early Progenitors")

# Ligand-Receptor Bubble Plot
# To identify specific signaling axes targeting early progenitors.
p_bubble <- netVisual_bubble(cellchat, sources.use = "Myeloid", remove.isolate = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Specific Ligand-Receptor Pairs from Myeloid Cells")
print(p_bubble)
dev.off() 

# 58. Data Export
# Export strictly filtered significant interactions for final manuscript documentation.
cat("Exporting key data to Excel...\n")
pathway_summary_filtered_df <- significant_interactions_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(pathway_name, source, target) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(total_interaction_strength = sum(prob), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(total_interaction_strength))
avg_expression_df <- as.data.frame(cellchat@data.signaling)
list_of_datasets_to_export <- list(
  "Significant_Interactions_p0.01" = significant_interactions_df,
  "Ranked_Pathways_p0.01"          = pathway_summary_filtered_df,
  "All_Interactions_Found"         = all_interactions_df, 
  "Avg_Gene_Expression"            = tibble::rownames_to_column(avg_expression_df, "gene")
)
write.xlsx(list_of_datasets_to_export, file = final_results_excel, rowNames = FALSE)
cat("\n\n--- FINAL FOCUSED ANALYSIS COMPLETE ---\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4M: TRANSCRIPTION FACTOR ACTIVITY PROFILING (VIPER + DoRothEA)
# ==============================================================================

# 59. Setup and Library Loading
# We utilize the DoRothEA regulon database and the VIPER algorithm to infer Transcription Factor (TF) activity based on the #expression of their downstream targets. This provides a more robust measure of regulatory state than mRNA levels alone.

# Define Paths (Standardized to Project Structure)
human_rds_path <- "./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds"
output_dir     <- "./Results"

# 60. Load Human Seurat Object and Standardize Identities
# Ensure the human maturation trajectory is correctly ordered (MEP -> Mature) for regulatory profiling.
cat("Loading Seurat object...\n")
human_seurat <- readRDS(human_rds_path)
custom_order_human <- c(
  "MEP", "BFU-E", "CFU-E", "Proerythroblast",
  "Basophilic Erythroblast", "Polychromatic Erythroblast",
  "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "Reticulocyte", "Mature erythrocyte-H"
)
human_seurat$cell_type <- plyr::mapvalues(
  x = as.character(human_seurat$seurat_clusters),
  from = as.character(c(5,1,2,8,6,0,4,9,10)),
  to   = custom_order_human
)
human_seurat$cell_type <- factor(human_seurat$cell_type, levels = custom_order_human)
Idents(human_seurat) <- "cell_type"

# 61. Load and Prepare DoRothEA Regulons
# We select regulons with confidence levels 'A' and 'B' (high confidence) 
# to ensure the inferred TF activities are supported by strong experimental evidence.
data(dorothea_hs, package = "dorothea")
regulons <- dorothea_hs %>% filter(confidence %in% c("A","B"))
tf_activities_regulons <- regulons %>%
  split(.$tf) %>%
  lapply(function(x){
    targets <- x$target
    mor <- x$mor
    names(mor) <- targets
    list(tfmode = mor, likelihood = rep(1, length(mor)))
  })

# 62. Run VIPER (Virtual Inference of Protein-activity by Enriched Regulon)
# Calculate TF activity scores for every single cell in the human erythroid lineage.
cat("Preparing expression matrix...\n")
expr_matrix <- as.matrix(GetAssayData(human_seurat, assay = "RNA", slot = "data"))
expr_matrix <- expr_matrix[is.finite(rowSums(expr_matrix)),]
cat("Running VIPER...\n")
tf_activity_scores <- viper(expr_matrix, tf_activities_regulons, minsize = 4, verbose = FALSE)
human_seurat[["viper"]] <- CreateAssayObject(data = tf_activity_scores)
DefaultAssay(human_seurat) <- "viper"
human_seurat <- ScaleData(human_seurat)

# 63. Identify Top 50 Variable Transcription Factors
# Quantify which TFs exhibit the greatest variance (shift in activity) 
# during the transition from progenitors to terminal maturation.
cat("Selecting top variable TFs...\n")
scaled_viper <- GetAssayData(human_seurat, assay = "viper", slot = "scale.data")
tf_variance <- apply(scaled_viper, 1, var)
top_50_tfs <- names(sort(tf_variance, decreasing = TRUE))[1:50]

# 64. Data Export (Supplementary Table 12)
# Provide a comprehensive single-cell activity table of the top 
# regulatory drivers for the manuscript.
cat("Exporting to Excel...\n")
tf_top50 <- as.data.frame(t(scaled_viper[top_50_tfs, ]))
tf_top50$Cell <- rownames(tf_top50)
tf_top50$Cell_Type <- human_seurat@meta.data$cell_type
tf_top50 <- tf_top50 %>% relocate(Cell, Cell_Type)
output_file <- file.path("./Results/Supplementary Table 12.xlsx")
openxlsx::write.xlsx(tf_top50, output_file, rowNames = FALSE)
cat("DONE!\nExcel saved at:\n", output_file, "\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4N: HUMAN-AVIAN ORTHOLOGY MAPPING (biomaRt)
# ==============================================================================

# 65. Generate Human-Chicken Ortholog Reference
# To perform high-confidence cross-species comparisons of regulatory fate , we generate a robust mapping of one-to-one orthologs 
# using the Ensembl biomaRt database
human_mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")
mart_data <- getBM(
  attributes = c('external_gene_name', 'ggallus_homolog_associated_gene_name'),
  mart = human_mart
)
colnames(mart_data) <- c("human_gene", "chicken_gene")
mart_data <- mart_data[mart_data$chicken_gene != "" & !is.na(mart_data$chicken_gene), ]
write.table(mart_data, 
            file = "./External_Data_File/Hsapiens_Ggallus_Ortholog_Mapping_Ensembl.txt", 
            sep = "\t", 
            row.names = FALSE, 
            quote = FALSE)
cat("Hsapiens_Ggallus_Ortholog_Mapping_Ensembl.txt created successfully.\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 4O: CROSS-SPECIES (Human - Chicken) REGULATORY TRAJECTORY 
# ==============================================================================

# 66. Comparative SRF Trajectory Analysis
# Based on the Transcription Factor Activity Profiling in SCRIPT 4M, 
# SRF (Serum Response Factor) was identified as a TF of interest as per our manuscript, its dynamic regulator in the human #lineage. Given its known role in governing the actin cytoskeleton and Vimentin, it represents the primary candidate for driving #the structural 

# 67. Load and Prepare Seurat Objects
human_rds_path <- "./Processed_Objects/HscRNA.rds"
chicken_rds_path <- "./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds"
ortholog_file_path <- "./External_Data_File/Hsapiens_Ggallus_Ortholog_Mapping_Ensembl.txt" 
target_tf <- "SRF"
cat("Loading Seurat objects...\n")
human_seurat <- readRDS(human_rds_path)
chicken_seurat <- readRDS(chicken_rds_path)
cat("Calculating VIPER activity for Human...\n")

# 68. Human Regulatory Activity Calculation (VIPER)
# To accurately capture the regulatory "crash" in the human lineage, 
# we calculate SRF activity via VIPER/DoRothEA, providing a protein-level 
# inference of regulatory fate.
data(dorothea_hs, package = "dorothea")
regulons <- dorothea_hs %>% filter(confidence %in% c("A","B"))
tf_activities_regulons <- regulons %>%
  split(.$tf) %>%
  lapply(function(x){
    targets <- x$target
    mor <- x$mor
    names(mor) <- targets
    list(tfmode = mor, likelihood = rep(1, length(mor)))
  })
expr_matrix <- as.matrix(GetAssayData(human_seurat, assay = "RNA", slot = "data"))
expr_matrix <- expr_matrix[is.finite(rowSums(expr_matrix)),]
tf_activity_scores <- viper(expr_matrix, tf_activities_regulons, minsize = 4, verbose = FALSE)
human_seurat[["viper"]] <- CreateAssayObject(data = tf_activity_scores)

# 69. Standardize Maturation Identifiers and Biological Order
# Synchronize the maturation timelines of both species to ensure 
# terminal maturation (Mature-C/H) are aligned on the X-axis.
custom_order_human <- c("MEP", "BFU-E", "CFU-E", "Proerythroblast", "Basophilic Erythroblast", "Polychromatic Erythroblast", "Orthochromatic Erythroblast", "Reticulocyte", "Mature erythrocyte-H")
human_seurat$cell_type <- plyr::mapvalues(x = as.character(human_seurat$seurat_clusters), from = as.character(c(5, 1, 2, 8, 6, 0, 4, 9, 10)), to = custom_order_human)
human_seurat$cell_type <- factor(human_seurat$cell_type, levels = custom_order_human)
Idents(human_seurat) <- "cell_type"
custom_order_chicken <- c("Erythroid progenitor 1", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Early erythroblast", "Late erythroblast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Mid polychromatic blast", "Late polychromatic blast", "Immature erythrocyte", "Mature erythrocyte-C")
chicken_seurat$cell_type <- plyr::mapvalues(x = as.character(chicken_seurat$seurat_clusters), from = as.character(c(5, 3, 8, 2, 4, 1, 0, 6, 9)), to = custom_order_chicken)
chicken_seurat$cell_type <- factor(chicken_seurat$cell_type, levels = custom_order_chicken)
Idents(chicken_seurat) <- "cell_type"

# 70. Map Cross-Species Orthology
# Use the mapping file created in Section 65 to identify the 
# specific SRF symbol in the Gallus gallus genome.
cat("Mapping ortholog for:", target_tf, "\n")
orthologs_df <- read.table(ortholog_file_path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
chicken_ortholog <- orthologs_df %>% filter(human_gene == target_tf) %>% pull(chicken_gene) %>% unique()
if(length(chicken_ortholog) == 0) chicken_ortholog <- target_tf
cat("Extracting trajectory data...\n")

# 71. Extract Trajectory Data
# Extract scaled intensity values (Z-scores) from both species 
# to enable direct comparison of different metrics (Activity vs Expression).
DefaultAssay(human_seurat) <- "viper"
human_data <- DotPlot(human_seurat, features = target_tf)$data %>%
  mutate(species = "Human", metric = "TF Activity (VIPER)")
DefaultAssay(chicken_seurat) <- "RNA"
chicken_data <- DotPlot(chicken_seurat, features = chicken_ortholog)$data %>%
  mutate(species = "Chicken", metric = "RNA Expression")
srf_trajectory_data <- rbind(human_data, chicken_data)
srf_trajectory_data$id <- factor(srf_trajectory_data$id, levels = c(custom_order_human, custom_order_chicken))

# 72. Generate Comparative Trajectory Plot
# Visualize the molecular divergence where human SRF activity 
# crashes during enucleation, while chicken SRF mRNA persists to support 
# the structural integrity of the mature nucleated cell.
cat("Generating plot...\n")
srf_plot <- ggplot(srf_trajectory_data, aes(x = id, y = avg.exp.scaled, group = species, color = species)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) + geom_point(size = 4) + 
  facet_grid(. ~ species, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Human" = "#de2d26", "Chicken" = "#3182bd")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = paste("Trajectory of", target_tf), 
       subtitle = "Human: VIPER Activity | Chicken: RNA Expression",
       y = "Relative Intensity (Scaled)")
pdf("./Results/Figure 5E.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
print(srf_plot)
dev.off()

cat("Success! Figure 5E saved to ./Results/ \n")
```
